channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Three adult victims, two suspects dead after shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego
item.description: Three victims are dead, as well as two suspected shooters after a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego.
item.content:encoded: Three victims are dead, as well as two suspected shooters after a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Kori Suzuki

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DRC draws attention to Trump administration's dismantling of USAID
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda concerns public health officials because of its size and because there have been so many cuts in global health.
item.content:encoded: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda concerns public health officials because of its size and because there have been so many cuts in global health.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: The All-American Rejects release first full-length album since 2012
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Tyson Ritter and Nick Wheeler of the All-American Rejects about their new album, Sandbox.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Tyson Ritter and Nick Wheeler of the All-American Rejects about their new album, Sandbox.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Scott

item.title: High school students learn historic building techniques
item.description: The U.S. has many buildings that are centuries old, but most modern tradespeople haven't learned how to work on them. A New Hampshire program is teaching high schoolers these old restoration skills.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. has many buildings that are centuries old, but most modern tradespeople haven't learned how to work on them. A New Hampshire program is teaching high schoolers these old restoration skills.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Harris

item.title: The business of storage units is booming. We met Vermont's operators
item.description: Self-storage operators in Vermont report long waitlists and big profits. The state's tight housing market and rapidly aging population contribute to the growth.
item.content:encoded: Self-storage operators in Vermont report long waitlists and big profits. The state's tight housing market and rapidly aging population contribute to the growth.
item.dc:creator: Mikaela Lefrak

item.title: Jury dismisses all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
item.description: A jury in a California courtroom has dismissed all claims in the case Elon Musk brought against Sam Altman and OpenAI.
item.content:encoded: A jury in a California courtroom has dismissed all claims in the case Elon Musk brought against Sam Altman and OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Myrow

item.title: Drake, in rebuilding mode, goes all in by releasing three albums at once
item.description: With his first major solo output since his battle with Kendrick Lamar in 2024 ended, Drake does what Drake has always done: puts out more music than most people could reasonably consume.
item.content:encoded: With his first major solo output since his battle with Kendrick Lamar in 2024 ended, Drake does what Drake has always done: puts out more music than most people could reasonably consume.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Carmichael

item.title: Through the American centuries, 'Amazing Grace' endures
item.description: The song, penned before American Independence by a slave trader turned abolitionist, has remained a source of comfort, inspiration and redemption.
item.content:encoded:
The song, penned before American Independence by a slave trader turned abolitionist, has remained a source of comfort, inspiration and redemption.

(Image credit: Eric Piper)
item.dc:creator: Lara Downes

item.title: Is Kennedy heir Jack Schlossberg ready to lead?
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Jack Schlossberg, Democratic candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District and the grandson of President John F. Kennedy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Jack Schlossberg, Democratic candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District and the grandson of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Mia Venkat

item.title: The National Weather Service is on a hiring spree ahead of hurricane season
item.description: As hurricane season approaches, the National Weather Service is on a hiring spree to make up for cuts it made last year under the DOGE initiative.
item.content:encoded: As hurricane season approaches, the National Weather Service is on a hiring spree to make up for cuts it made last year under the DOGE initiative.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Staletovich
